zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Joan Marsh於1535年出生在英國越可爾馬波萊頓時，她的父親Sir William John Marsh21歲，母親Mary Ann Milborne15歲。她於1557年與Sir John Gibbons of East Yorkshire在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她於1559年住在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾瑟格利。她去世於1578年在英國越可爾約克享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Joan Marsh于1535年出生在英国越可爾馬波萊頓时，她的父亲Sir William John Marsh21岁，母亲Mary Ann Milborne15岁。她于1557年与Sir John Gibbons of East Yorkshire在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她于1559年住在英国英格兰斯塔福爾瑟格利。她去世于1578年在英国越可爾約克享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: Marsh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by or in a marsh or fen, Middle English mersch (Old English mersc), or a habitational name from any of various minor places called with this word, for example in Yorkshire, Shropshire and Buckinghamshire.
Americanized form of Slovenian Marš: unexplained. Compare Mars 7.

variants.name: Marsch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin.
North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a marsh or fen, Middle Low German marsch.

variants.name: Mash
meanings: English: variant of Marsh with loss of -r-.
Americanized form of German   Masch  , in part of Sorbian origin.

variants.name: Masch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Erwin, Horst, Ralf.  Jewish Ber, Sima.
North German: topographic name from Middle Low German masch ‘marsh, fen’, a variant of marsch. Compare English Marsh .
German (Alemannic; Mäsch): nickname from Middle High German mast ‘fat, stout’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Joan Marsh was born in 1535, in Mappleton, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir William John Marsh, was 21 and her mother, Mary Ann Milborne, was 15. She married Sir John Gibbons of East Yorkshire in 1557, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She lived in Sedgley, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom in 1559. She died in 1578, in York, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 43.

Elle a épousé Sir John Gibbons of East Yorkshire en 1557, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle a vécu à Sedgley, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni en 1559. Elle est décédée en 1578, à York, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 43 ans.

ko.Mappleton, Yorkshire East Riding, Englan: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 마플턴
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Marsh(이)가 1535년 영국 요르크실르 마플턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William John Marsh(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Ann Milborne(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1557년 영국 요르크실르에서 Sir John Gibbons of East Yorkshire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1559년도에 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 세지리에서 살았다. 그녀는 1578년 영국 요르크실르 요르크에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.
